improvements.point: You already have excellent flow and speed.
improvements.suggestion: Keep practicing speaking on many different topics to make sure your flow stays this good, even with very difficult ideas.

strengths.point: You speak at a very fast and natural speed.
strengths.quote: Your speed was 204 words per minute.

strengths.point: You speak continuously without any pauses.
strengths.quote: You had no long pauses in your speech.

strengths.point: Your speech flows very smoothly, like a native speaker.
strengths.quote: For the most part, I think, going to university is a waste of time.

FC.summary: You speak with exceptional fluency and at a very fast pace. Your speech is continuous and smooth, showing no hesitation or pauses.

strengths.point: You use a variety of subordinate adverbial clauses to add detail and context to your statements.
strengths.quote: ...when you go into the real world and you start to do a job.

strengths.point: You effectively use relative clauses to provide specific information about nouns.
strengths.quote: Most of the subjects that you study are pretty useless...

strengths.point: You skillfully employ noun clauses to express your opinions and feelings.
strengths.quote: I feel that you don't need to be an excellent student.

strengths.point: You use gerunds as subjects and objects of prepositions to discuss actions and activities.
strengths.quote: picking a university that has a name is more important than the grades.

GRA.summary: You use a wide range of grammatical structures flexibly and accurately. Your sentences are frequently error-free, and you effectively combine both simple and complex forms to express your ideas clearly.

improvements.point: You sometimes repeat the same phrases.
improvements.suggestion: Try to use different ways to say 'for the most part' or 'real world' to show more variety in your vocabulary.

strengths.point: You use natural, common phrases that make your speech sound fluent.
You use specific words to talk about education.
You use clear and direct language to express your feelings.

LR.summary: You use a good range of everyday words to talk about the topic clearly. Your vocabulary helps you express your ideas well and is easy to understand.

improvements.point: Sometimes, you mispronounce certain sounds, which can occasionally affect clarity.
improvements.suggestion: Pay attention to sounds like the 't' in 'most' and 'part', and the 'p' in 'part'. Practice these sounds to make them clearer.

improvements.point: While your word stress is generally good, there are a few instances where the stress is placed on the wrong syllable.
improvements.suggestion: Practice stressing the correct syllable in words like 'sometimes' (stress the first syllable: SOME-times) to improve natural rhythm.

improvements.point: Although you link words well, there are opportunities to link more consonant-to-vowel sounds to enhance the flow of your speech.
improvements.suggestion: Try to link the final consonant of a word to the starting vowel of the next word, for example, in 'picking a' and 'more important'.

strengths.point: You pronounce most individual sounds clearly and accurately, making your speech very easy to follow.
You generally place stress correctly on words, which helps with the natural flow of your speech.
You link words together well, especially consonant-to-vowel sounds, which makes your speech sound natural and connected.

PN.summary: Your pronunciation is very clear and easy to understand, with your accent having only a minimal effect on intelligibility. You use a good range of pronunciation features effectively.

improvements.point: You need to consider more than one point of view.
improvements.suggestion: When the question asks you to consider various viewpoints, try to explain different perspectives on the topic, not just your own.

improvements.point: You need to develop your ideas more fully.
improvements.suggestion: Explain your reasons in more detail. For example, you could give specific examples of why subjects are useless or how colleges are not like the real world.

strengths.point: You clearly state your main opinion on the topic.
strengths.quote: going to university is a waste of time.

strengths.point: You provide reasons to support your viewpoint.
strengths.quote: subjects that you study are pretty useless

strengths.point: You address both parts of the question.
strengths.quote: you can develop your character, but I fear...

TR.summary: You clearly state your opinion on the topic and provide reasons to support your ideas. You address both statements in the question, but you could explore different viewpoints more deeply.
